description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Rename the published OpenWork server package to openwork-server so install, publish, and version checks all use the same npm package name.
- Polish the session trace row so the summary, icon, and chevron stay visually aligned and easier to scan.
Fixes & polish
- Update orchestrator, release, and headless-web tooling to resolve and verify the renamed server package consistently.
- Tighten trace row spacing and baseline alignment across the recent session trace UI fixes.
Packaging / ops
- Roll the workspace forward to v0.11.172 across app, desktop, orchestrator, router, and server packages.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.171...v0.11.172
